title: Ranunculus variantifrons
taxonomy: Ranunculus variantifrons is classified within the family Ranunculaceae, commonly known as the buttercup family. This plant is part of the Ranunculus genus, which comprises over 600 species of flowering plants. side_effects: Ranunculus variantifrons is a plant that can be toxic if ingested, and its sap can cause skin irritation and allergic reactions in some individuals. intro: Ranunculus variantifrons, commonly known as a species of buttercup, is a flowering plant in the Ranunculaceae family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiseptic properties, offering potential health benefits in treating various conditions such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues.
